locations.heading: Definition: Feature Equality
locations.excerpt: This specification defines two Features as equal if both Features point to the same exact contents and are executed with the same options.

locations.heading: Definition: Feature Equality

locations.heading: Docker Compose based
locations.excerpt: `service`: declares the **main** container that will be used for all other operations.

locations.heading: Users
locations.excerpt: Set using the `remoteUser` property in all cases and defaults to the container user.

locations.heading: workspaceFolder and workspaceMount
locations.excerpt: The default mount point for the source code can be set with the `workspaceMount` property for **image** and **dockerfile** scenarios or using the built in `mounts` property in **Docker Compose** files.

locations.heading: Image Metadata
locations.excerpt: The metadata is added to the image as a `devcontainer.metadata` label with a JSON string value representing the above array or single object.

locations.heading: Dockerfile based
locations.excerpt: The only required parameter in this case is the relative reference to the `Dockerfile` in `build.dockerfile`.

locations.heading: Image based
locations.excerpt: The only required parameter is `image`.

locations.heading: Project Workspace Folder
locations.excerpt: The **project workspace folder** is where an implementing tool should begin to search for `devcontainer.json` files.

locations.excerpt: Union of all ports without duplicates. Last one wins (when mapping changes).

locations.heading: Passing secrets in
locations.excerpt: A conforming implementation should provide a secure mechanism to pass secrets, such as a secrets file, Windows credential manager, Mac keychain, Azure keyvault etc. for example.

locations.heading: Proposal
locations.excerpt: A [supporting tool](https://containers.dev/supporting#tools) (e.g. the [dev container CLI reference implementation](https://github.com/devcontainers/cli)) should behave according to the following properties:

locations.heading: Example
locations.excerpt: "PASSWORD": "Simple Passwords"

locations.heading: Passing secrets in
locations.excerpt: Secrets are not part of dev containers specification and we do not expect users to store secrets inside `devcontainer.json` file.

locations.heading: What are secrets
locations.excerpt: Users can change a secret's value at any time, and a conforming dev containers implementation should support dynamically changing secrets without having to rebuild the container.

locations.heading: GitHub Codespaces
locations.excerpt: A [codespace](https://docs.github.com/en/codespaces/overview) is a development environment that's hosted in the cloud.

locations.heading: Dev Container CLI
locations.excerpt: A dev container command line interface (CLI) that implements this specification.

locations.heading: Visual Studio Code
locations.excerpt: An array of extension IDs that specify the extensions that should be installed inside the container when it is created. Defaults to `[]`.

locations.heading: Product specific properties
Product specific properties
locations.excerpt: Codespaces will automatically perform some default setup when the `devcontainer.json` does not specify a `postCreateCommand`.

locations.heading: Visual Studio Code Dev Containers
locations.excerpt: lets you use a container as a full-featured development environment.

locations.heading: Product specific limitations
locations.excerpt: | `mounts` | array | Codespaces ignores "bind" mounts with the exception of the Docker socket. Volume mounts are still allowed.|

locations.heading: Visual Studio Code
Product specific limitations
locations.excerpt: | `shutdownAction` | enum | Does not apply to Codespaces. |

locations.heading: Visual Studio
locations.excerpt: Visual Studio added dev container support in Visual Studio 2022 17.4 for C++ projects using CMake Presets.

locations.heading: Visual Studio Code
locations.excerpt: Visual Studio Code specific properties go under `vscode` inside `customizations`.

locations.heading: The optionalPaths property
locations.excerpt: A path is relative to the root of the Template source directory.

locations.heading: Folder structure
locations.excerpt: A single Template is a folder with at least a `devcontainer-template.json` and

locations.heading: Option Resolution
locations.excerpt: A supporting tool will parse the `options` object provided by the user. If a value is selected for a Template, it will be replaced in the files (within the sub-directory of the Template).

locations.heading: The optionalPaths property
locations.excerpt: Before applying a Template, tooling must inspect the `optionalPaths` property of a Template and prompt the user on whether each file or folder should be included in the resulting output workspace folder.

locations.heading: OCI Registry
locations.excerpt: A custom media type `application/vnd.devcontainers` and `application/vnd.devcontainers.layer.v1+tar` are used as demonstrated below.

locations.heading: Source code
locations.excerpt: A Template's source code is stored in a git repository.

locations.heading: OCI Registry
locations.excerpt: Each packaged Template is pushed to the registry following the naming convention `<registry>/<namespace>/<id>[:version]`, where version is the major, minor, and patch version of the Template, according to the semver specification.

locations.heading: Source code
locations.excerpt: Each sub-directory should be named such that it matches the `id` field of the `devcontainer-template.json`.

locations.heading: devcontainer-collection.json
locations.excerpt: Each Template's `devcontainer-template.json` metadata file is appended into the `templates` top-level array.

locations.heading: Source code
locations.excerpt: Templates and [Features](./devcontainer-features.md) should be placed in different git repositories.

locations.heading: Packaging
locations.excerpt: Templates are distributed as tarballs.

locations.heading: OCI Registry
locations.excerpt: The collection file is always tagged as `latest`.

locations.heading: Packaging
locations.excerpt: The tarball is named `devcontainer-template-<id>.tgz`, where `<id>` is the Templates's `id` field.

locations.heading: Versioning
locations.excerpt: Tooling that handles publishing Templates will not republish Templates if that exact version has already been published; however, tooling must republish major and minor versions in accordance with the semver specification.

locations.heading: The optionalPaths property
locations.excerpt: For a directory, provide the full relative path with a trailing slash and asterisk (`/*`) appended to the path.  The directory and its children will be recursively ignored.

locations.heading: Option resolution example
locations.excerpt: "image": "mcr.microsoft.com/devcontainers/java:0-${templateOption:imageVariant}",

locations.heading: The options property
locations.excerpt: "proposals": ["value1", "value2"],

locations.excerpt: The `devcontainer-template.json` file defines information about the Template to be used by any

locations.heading: The options property
locations.excerpt: The `options` must be unique for every `devcontainer-template.json`

locations.heading: The options property
locations.excerpt: The tools would replace the option ID with the selected value in all the files (within the sub-directory of the Template).

locations.heading: The options property
locations.excerpt: This replacement would happen before dropping the `.devcontainer/devcontainer.json` and other files (within the sub-directory of the Template) required to containerize your project.

locations.heading: Versioning
locations.excerpt: Tooling that handles releasing Templates will not republish Templates if that exact version has already been published; however, tooling must republish major and minor versions in accordance with the semver specification.
